summary: PR #2589 was a narrow configure.py linkage-status change titled "link some free files". It changed several object entries from NonMatching to Matching, removed one NonMatching ft_3C61.c entry, and produced a bot report showing linked code/data increases. The PR was closed without a recorded merge in the supplied metadata, and a final issue comment said the matter was being handled in a separate PR.

key_files.role: Only changed file; MatchingFor object list was updated to mark several source files as Matching and remove one NonMatching object entry.

what_changed.text: Changed Object status from NonMatching to Matching for melee/pl/plattack.c.
Changed Object status from NonMatching to Matching for melee/pl/plbonus.c.
Removed the Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_3C61.c") entry from the MatchingFor list.
Changed Object status from NonMatching to Matching for melee/ft/ftchangeparam.c.
Changed Object status from NonMatching to Matching for melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.c.
Changed Object status from NonMatching to Matching for sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c.
The decomp bot report for GALE01 showed linked code increasing by 35120 bytes and linked data by 824 bytes.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the edit surface narrow: only configure.py changed, with no source-body edits in the supplied diff.

decomp_lessons.text: A link-only PR can improve linked-code metrics by changing object status in configure.py, but the PR evidence here does not document the underlying source matching work or verification details for the individual files.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use configure.py Object status flips from NonMatching to Matching to link files once they are considered free/matching; in this PR the tactic was applied to plattack.c, plbonus.c, ftchangeparam.c, ftPp_SpecialS.c, and bytecode.c.
Removing a NonMatching object entry can also affect linkage composition; this PR removed the ft_3C61.c NonMatching entry, but the evidence does not explain why.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.text: Handling this in a separate PR.

follow_up_queries: PR 2589 separate PR Handling this in a separate PR
configure.py plattack.c plbonus.c ftchangeparam.c ftPp_SpecialS.c bytecode.c Matching
ft_3C61.c removed from configure.py NonMatching PR

notes: Strong evidence for the exact configure.py diff and changed object-status entries.
The PR body is empty in text_corpus.jsonl; the only human text is an issue comment saying the work is being handled separately.
No review comments or reviews were included.
No source-body changes, objdiff output, per-file match proof, or rationale for the ft_3C61.c removal are present in the slice.
Metadata says author BR-, while human_pr_text.md labels the displayed excerpt author as PsiLupan because it is an issue comment, not the PR author.
Metadata records state CLOSED and merged_at null, so this intake does not treat the change as merged or accepted.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Link-only configure.py PRs need separate evidence for underlying matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2589 shows configure.py Object entries being changed from NonMatching to Matching and a linked-code/data bot report, but the slice does not include the source recovery work or per-file verification. Curators should treat such PRs as linkage-status evidence rather than proof of specific decomp tactics inside the listed source files.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2589 touched configure.py MatchingFor object status entries
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2589, configure.py changed plattack.c, plbonus.c, ftchangeparam.c, ftPp_SpecialS.c, and sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c from NonMatching to Matching, and removed the NonMatching ft_3C61.c entry.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a possible historical path fact about configure.py linkage status changes, but it should be curated separately because the PR was closed unmerged in the supplied metadata and no underlying match verification is included.

rejection_notes: Do not infer naming conventions from this PR; no names were changed in source files.
Do not infer function-level decomp tactics for plattack.c, plbonus.c, ftchangeparam.c, ftPp_SpecialS.c, or bytecode.c; the slice only changes configure.py status entries.
Do not mark the PR as merged or accepted; merged_at is null in the supplied metadata.
Do not treat the ft_3C61.c removal as explained beyond the raw diff; no rationale is present.
